texto: Every man, every woman, every child has some talent, some power, some opportunity of getting good and doing good. Each day offers some occasion for using this talent. As we use it, it gradually increases, improves, becomes native to the character. As we neglect it, it dwindles, withers, and disappears. This is the stern but benign law by which we live.
explicacao: Citação de James Clarke sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “Every man, every woman, every child has some talent, some power, some opportunity of getting good…” articula reflexao, talent, every em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.
